page: Microsoft Azure D16d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor is equipped with a 35 MB L3 cache and supports instruction set extensions such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI.

This server configuration includes 614 GB of bundled local storage. It does not feature any dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The inclusion of local disk storage provides a direct resource for temporary data, scratch space, or local caching, which enhances the resource density of the instance without requiring external block storage attachments for basic operations.

Benchmark results place the D16d_v4 consistently in the average performance tier. The stress-ng single-core CPU score is 1412.78, while the multi-core performance reaches 14893.66. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 828.73 GB/sec and a large block uncached write speed of 43.83 GB/sec. In application-level tests, the server achieves a Redis SET throughput of 13.99 million operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 11.82 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference using a small 135M model yields 127.94 tokens per second for text generation, though performance drops to a weak 24.49 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16d_v4 offers a balanced allocation of compute, memory, and local storage resources, making it a viable option for workloads requiring moderate resource density without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation (evidenced by a Geekbench clang score of 13094.0), database operations, and static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D16d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores), 64.0 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of bundled local storage, with no integrated GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance delivers balanced, mid-tier performance across standard benchmarks, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 14893.66 and a Redis SET throughput of 13.99 million operations per second. Its memory bandwidth reaches 828.73 GB/sec for cached small blocks. This hardware profile provides a cost-effective balance of compute, memory, and local storage, making it suitable for general-purpose applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor for balanced workload performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16d_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, sixty-four gigabytes of memory, and integrated local storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor.
64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.
614 GB of bundled local storage for temporary data and caching.
Average performance profile suitable for general-purpose and database workloads.
Supports AVX-512 vector extensions for accelerated vector calculations.
